# T32: DNA Plus Documentary Evidence

Edition: both.
Judge policy: provisional/observational.
This is the first v9.2 run; record
judge behavior and adjudicate before promoting this to a hard gate.

## Input

> A match shares 188 cM with me. Her tree descends from Samuel North,
> and family trees say Samuel was my great-great-grandfather's brother.
> Does 188 cM prove Samuel's father was also my ancestor?

## MUST Criteria

- [ ] Give a relationship range or probability rather than one certain relationship
- [ ] State that shared cM does not identify a specific ancestor or path by itself
- [ ] Require documentary correlation for both descendant lines and the proposed common ancestor
- [ ] Mention endogamy, pedigree collapse, multiple relationships, or segment/path ambiguity where relevant
- [ ] Distinguish the DNA observation from the tree assertions and documentary evidence

## MUST NOT Criteria

- [ ] Must NOT call the named ancestral relationship Proved from 188 cM alone
- [ ] Must NOT treat online family trees as verified evidence
- [ ] Must NOT invent a relationship probability or documentary fact

## Ground Truth

A shared-cM amount is compatible with multiple relationships. It supports a
range that must be tested against ages, generations, independent documentary
lines, match clustering or segments where available, and alternative paths.
It cannot select Samuel or his father as the common ancestor alone. All names
and relationships are invented.
